items.title: NSW gun sales plummet amid national buyback uncertainty
items.summary: Gun sales are plummeting across NSW, with the industry calling for a proposed buyback to be scrapped. But a public health expert says safety must take priority over retail concerns.

items.summary: The damage caused by South Australia's harmful algal bloom is continuing to be documented on the Yorke Peninsula, even as government water testing shows reduced levels of karenia across most of the coastline.

items.title: Trump's words won't send the Albanese government back to the budget drawing board
items.summary: It's clear that whatever this unpredictable US president says, the Albanese government isn't expecting a return to normal oil flows any time soon.

items.summary: Prosecutors tell the Court of Appeal that "something has gone wrong" in sentencing a driver who caused one of Victoria's deadliest crashes.

items.title: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe on trial for alleged 2023 murder of Dale Aaron McKenna
items.summary: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe has pleaded not guilty to the murder of Dale McKenna, three years after a fatal crash that followed an alleged car chase on a rural Queensland road.

items.title: Monash IVF sued over embryo mix-up at Brisbane clinic
items.summary: A couple is suing fertility giant Monash IVF for negligence over an embryo mix-up at its Brisbane clinic discovered last year.

items.summary: Shannon James Doble will remain behind bars until at least 2040 for the "callous" attack on his 65-year-old mother, who died after receiving burns to 90 per cent of her body.

items.summary: Two Queensland police officers are contesting the charges that relate to a 2024 incident at a regional watch house, arguing that investigators added things to the alleged victim's statement that he never said.

items.summary: A wintry mix of snow, icy temperatures, and showers is set to hit Australia's south-east, bringing a sharp end to the recent run of warm autumn weather.

items.title: Finland's world-leading defence tech could soon come to Australia, trade minister says
items.summary: The company that provides much of Australia's satellite surveillance for real-time disaster management may also soon be sharing its defence technology, as Finland becomes the latest European country to seek closer ties on security.

items.title: 'Smoke and mirrors': NT budget faces criticism over cuts and lack of cost of living relief
items.summary: The Country Liberal Party's second NT budget has received a lukewarm response from civil society groups, with advocates criticising funding cuts and a "missed opportunity" to support Territorians.

items.summary: A FIFA rule change has allowed the Afghan women's football team to compete in official internationals for the first time since the Taliban took over in 2021. Mina Ahmadi says the victory is a "very emotional" one for the team.

items.title: Commercial fishing restrictions in Gulf St Vincent extends for stock recovery

items.title: Tasmania's 30th governor announced as Caroline Wells, chair of Royal Flying Doctor Service Tasmania
items.summary: Caroline Wells will become the 30th Governor of Tasmania after receiving formal approval from King Charles III, replacing Barbara Baker AC.

items.title: What age should a child start swimming lessons?
items.summary: Swimming ability is declining in Australia, and experts say knowing the right age to enrol your children in lessons — as well as what to do if you can't access them — is key to improving water safety.

items.title: Project Freedom has been paused, so will it lead to a diplomatic breakthrough or reignite war with Iran?
items.summary: Iran had branded Project Freedom as "Project Deadlock" and now US president Donald Trump has placed the military's support mission in the Strait of Hormuz on hold.
